refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct obligations: (1) deriving requirements from context, (2) recording them, and (3) managing them over time. Derivation is the primary functional obligation in the problem domain—it answers 'what must the system do to translate mission inputs into performance targets?' Recording and managing are secondary concerns that belong to separate atomic FRs. The refined FR is solution-neutral: it does not prescribe a database, document format, or management tool.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate that given a worksite mission context (e.g., site layout, production schedule, safety constraints), the system produces a documented set of performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) that are traceable to the input context and accepted by the Site Manager and Safety Manager.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from worksite mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.2: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements in a persistent, retrievable format.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The NGHE solution shall maintain a history of performance requirement baselines and calibration updates with change traceability.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The NGHE solution shall ensure performance requirement derivation is traceable to worksite inputs and documented for audit.
refined_fr.note: The original FR is composite. The refined primary FR addresses derivation only. Sub-FRs 005.2–005.4 address recording, history management, and traceability as separate atomic concerns, each of which will map to distinct DPs and IMs.

candidate_dps: DP1: Capture worksite mission context (safety plan, operational policies, environmental constraints) via structured intake process; parse into a canonical context model; apply context-to-performance mapping rules (logical inference engine) to derive initial performance targets; store targets with traceability metadata (source context element → target).

DP2: Maintain a versioned baseline registry (performance targets + calibration state) with immutable audit trail; on each update, record delta (what changed, why, when, who); expose baseline history and current state via query interface; support rollback to prior baseline if needed.

DP3: Decompose worksite mission into functional domains (safety, productivity, environmental); assign performance metrics to each domain independently; aggregate domain metrics into system-level targets; maintain explicit mapping between domain metrics and worksite operational policies.

DP4: Implement a two-phase derivation process: (Phase 1) extract raw mission parameters from worksite context; (Phase 2) validate and reconcile parameters against acceptance criteria (traceability, completeness, consistency); flag unresolved or conflicting inputs for human review before finalizing targets.

DP5: Establish a context-to-performance mapping table (lookup or rule-based) that translates worksite inputs (e.g., 'high-risk environment', 'continuous operation') into quantified performance requirements; version the mapping table; allow customization per worksite class or mission type.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2 + DP4 (integrated): Maintain a versioned baseline registry with immutable audit trail (DP2) coupled with a two-phase derivation and validation process (DP4). Phase 1 extracts and structures worksite mission context into a canonical model; Phase 2 validates completeness and traceability, then derives performance targets via context-to-performance rules. All targets and baselines are recorded with full provenance (source context → target, timestamp, actor, rationale). History is queryable and supports rollback.

potential_sources: DP2 and DP4 are logically coupled but appropriately so: DP4 (derivation + validation) feeds into DP2 (baseline registry). The coupling is unidirectional (DP4 → DP2) and supports the acceptance criterion of traceability.

Risk: If context-to-performance mapping rules (DP4 Phase 2) are tightly bound to specific worksite types or policies, generalizability may suffer. Mitigation: parameterize the mapping rules and version them separately from the baseline registry.
Risk: Audit trail (DP2) could become a bottleneck if not indexed properly. Mitigation: design the history store with efficient query support (e.g., by timestamp, actor, target ID).
coupling_check.notes: DP2 + DP4 avoids tight coupling to specific technologies (sensor types, protocols, vendors). The design is solution-neutral and supports independence: the context intake, derivation logic, and baseline storage can be implemented in separate modules with well-defined interfaces. Traceability is explicit and maintained throughout.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite mission context is available in a structured or semi-structured form (e.g., safety plan document, operational policy document). Risk: If context is unstructured or incomplete, Phase 1 parsing may fail or require manual intervention. Mitigation: define a canonical context schema and require worksites to provide inputs conforming to it.

Assumption: Context-to-performance mapping rules are stable and well-understood for the target worksite classes. Risk: If rules are ad-hoc or domain-specific, they may not generalize. Mitigation: establish a formal rule definition process and version rules independently.

Assumption: Acceptance criteria (traceability, history, calibration updates) can be satisfied by a versioned registry with audit trail. Risk: If regulatory or compliance requirements demand cryptographic proof of integrity, a simple audit trail may be insufficient. Mitigation: design the baseline registry to support optional cryptographic signing or blockchain-style hashing if needed.

Assumption: Rollback to prior baselines is always safe and desirable. Risk: If performance targets are interdependent or have side effects, rollback could introduce inconsistency. Mitigation: validate rollback targets for consistency before applying.

Assumption: Worksite operational policies are static or change infrequently. Risk: If policies change rapidly, the derived performance targets may become stale. Mitigation: implement a policy change notification mechanism and trigger re-derivation when policies change.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine the selected DP (DP2 + DP4) by explicitly separating concerns: (1) Context Intake & Parsing (DP4 Phase 1): define a canonical worksite context schema and a parser that converts unstructured inputs into structured context objects. (2) Derivation & Validation (DP4 Phase 2): implement context-to-performance mapping as a parameterized rule engine; version rules separately from baselines. (3) Baseline Registry (DP2): design as a versioned, queryable store with immutable audit trail; support rollback with consistency checks. (4) Traceability: ensure every performance target carries metadata linking it to source context elements and derivation rules. This separation enables independent evolution of context schemas, mapping rules, and storage mechanisms, reducing coupling and improving testability. Additionally, introduce an explicit IM (Implementation Module) layer: designate separate modules for (a) Context Parser, (b) Rule Engine, (c) Baseline Store, and (d) Audit Logger, each with clear ownership and interfaces.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized baseline registry, versioned audit trail, two-phase derivation pipeline (context extraction → canonical model → validation → performance target derivation), and provenance tracking for all mission-critical parameters and their historical evolution.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Ingest worksite mission context (environmental, operational, safety constraints) to populate Phase 1 context extraction and feed validation rules in Phase 2.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Emit derived performance targets, baseline parameters, and rollback directives to downstream execution systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services).

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receive safety constraint updates that trigger re-validation and re-derivation; emit audit alerts when baseline changes occur or rollback is executed.

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent fit. NGHE Solution is the logical home for mission-level baseline management, versioned registry, audit trail, and derivation logic. Phase 1 (context → canonical model) and Phase 2 (validation → target derivation) are inherently solution-level concerns. Provenance tracking and rollback are core NGHE responsibilities. Minor diffusion: Telemetry ingestion and Tasking emission require external interface contracts, but logic remains contained.

Poor fit. Safety Services is specialized for real-time hazard detection and alert generation, not baseline registry or derivation pipelines. Placing versioned audit trail and two-phase derivation here would fragment mission-level concerns. Safety constraints would be inputs to NGHE derivation, not owners of it. High diffusion: performance target logic, context canonicalization, and provenance would leak into Vehicle and Teleoperation.

Very poor fit. Vehicle is a physical/execution platform, not a governance or planning layer. Baseline registry, audit trail, and derivation pipelines belong at the solution level, not embedded in a single vehicle. Massive diffusion: multi-vehicle missions would require replication or cross-vehicle synchronization. Versioning and rollback would be vehicle-specific rather than mission-global.

Very poor fit. Teleoperation is an operator interface and command pathway, not a baseline or derivation engine. Placing versioned registry and two-phase validation here conflates operator control with mission governance. High diffusion: performance targets and audit trails would need to propagate to Vehicle and Safety Services independently. Provenance tracking would be operator-centric rather than mission-centric.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The integrated DP (versioned baseline registry + two-phase derivation with full provenance) is a mission-governance concern that naturally belongs at the solution level. NGHE Solution is the authoritative owner of mission context, baseline parameters, and derived performance targets. All downstream modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) consume targets and constraints from NGHE, not the reverse. This containment preserves single-source-of-truth semantics and enables consistent rollback and audit across all mission participants.
containment_risks: Risk: If Telemetry ingestion is high-volume or real-time, Phase 1 context extraction may become a bottleneck. Mitigation: Implement asynchronous, batched context ingestion with caching; separate real-time telemetry streams from mission-context updates.

Risk: Two-phase validation may introduce latency in target derivation. If missions require rapid re-baselining, Phase 2 validation could block Tasking emission. Mitigation: Implement incremental validation and cached rule evaluation; support fast-path derivation for non-critical parameter updates.

Risk: Provenance tracking (source context → target, timestamp, actor, rationale) may accumulate large audit logs. Querying and rollback performance could degrade. Mitigation: Implement tiered storage (hot audit log in NGHE, cold archive external); index by mission ID, timestamp, and parameter type.

Risk: Rollback semantics must be carefully defined: does rollback affect only future targets or also in-flight tasks? Ambiguity could cause Safety Services and Vehicle to diverge. Mitigation: Define explicit rollback scope (e.g., 'future targets only' vs. 'abort in-flight tasks') and emit SafetyAlert notifications on rollback.

Risk: If multiple NGHE instances exist (e.g., for redundancy or multi-site operations), baseline registry consistency becomes critical. Mitigation: Implement distributed consensus or primary-replica pattern with explicit synchronization guarantees.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP2 + DP4 (integrated versioned baseline registry + two-phase derivation with full provenance) directly and sufficiently addresses FR requirement to derive performance requirements from worksite mission context. The DP explicitly covers context extraction (Phase 1), canonical modeling, validation (Phase 2), target derivation via context-to-performance rules, and immutable audit trail with queryable history. Traceability from worksite inputs to performance targets is architecturally sound. No logical gaps identified.

diffusion_risks: MINOR: Telemetry ingestion interface (inbound) and Tasking emission interface (outbound) create external coupling points. Risk is low because logic remains contained in NGHE Solution, but interface contracts must be versioned and validated to prevent downstream misinterpretation of baseline changes.

MODERATE: SafetyAlert bidirectional interface introduces feedback loop. Safety constraint updates trigger re-validation and re-derivation in NGHE, but audit alerts emitted back to Safety Services could create circular dependencies if Safety Services uses those alerts to re-trigger constraint updates. Mitigation: Define explicit trigger conditions and prevent re-entrant validation cycles.

MODERATE: Provenance tracking (source context → target, timestamp, actor, rationale) is correctly contained in NGHE Solution, but if audit log queries become frequent or high-volume, the IM boundary may need to expose a dedicated audit query interface. Risk: Uncontrolled query patterns could leak implementation details. Mitigation: Provide bounded, schema-versioned audit query API.

LOW: Rollback directive emission (Tasking interface) requires careful state synchronization with Vehicle and Teleoperation. If rollback scope is ambiguous (future targets vs. abort in-flight tasks), downstream modules may interpret directives inconsistently. Mitigation: Embed explicit rollback scope and timestamp in Tasking messages.

LOW: Multi-instance NGHE deployments (redundancy, multi-site) could fragment baseline registry consistency. Mitigation: Enforce primary-replica or distributed consensus pattern with explicit synchronization guarantees documented in IM interface contracts.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Excellent containment (0.92 score). NGHE Solution is the authoritative, single-source-of-truth owner of mission-level baseline management, versioned registry, audit trail, and derivation logic. Phase 1 (context extraction → canonical model) and Phase 2 (validation → target derivation) are inherently solution-level governance concerns. Provenance tracking and rollback are core NGHE responsibilities. All downstream modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) consume targets and constraints from NGHE; none own or replicate the derivation pipeline. This preserves single-source-of-truth semantics and enables consistent, auditable baseline evolution across all mission participants. Minor diffusion is limited to external interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert), which is appropriate and manageable.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: HIGH. Acceptance criteria are directly verifiable: (1) 'Documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs' — NGHE Solution's provenance tracking (source context → target, timestamp, actor, rationale) provides full traceability; audit log queries can reconstruct derivation chain for any target. (2) 'History of baselines/calibration updates' — versioned baseline registry with immutable audit trail directly satisfies this; queryable history supports compliance audits. (3) Alignment with 'Worksite safety plan and operational policies' — Phase 2 validation rules can encode safety plan constraints and policy requirements; re-validation on SafetyAlert updates ensures ongoing compliance. Test strategy: (a) Inject worksite mission context via Telemetry; verify Phase 1 extraction and canonical model correctness. (b) Validate Phase 2 completeness checks and rule-based derivation against known worksite scenarios. (c) Query audit trail for arbitrary target; verify full provenance chain is reconstructible. (d) Execute rollback; verify audit log records rollback event, scope, and actor. (e) Trigger SafetyAlert; verify re-validation and re-derivation occur and are logged.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: CLEAR. Functional allocation is unambiguous: NGHE Solution owns mission-level baseline derivation and governance; Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are consumers of derived targets and constraints. Ownership boundaries are well-defined by the IM containment analysis (0.92 score for NGHE, <0.5 for all alternatives). Interface contracts (Telemetry inbound, Tasking outbound, SafetyAlert bidirectional) are explicit and directional. Responsibility for Phase 1 context extraction, Phase 2 validation, provenance tracking, and rollback execution is unambiguously assigned to NGHE Solution. No ambiguity regarding who owns the versioned baseline registry or audit trail. Downstream modules have clear, bounded responsibilities: Vehicle executes targets; Teleoperation relays operator commands; Safety Services monitors hazards and updates constraints. Cross-module dependencies are minimal and flow in one direction (NGHE → consumers).

recommended_revisions: REVISION 1 (Interface Contracts): Formalize Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert interface specifications with explicit schema versioning, message formats, and error handling. Define rollback scope semantics (e.g., 'future targets only', 'abort in-flight tasks', 'advisory only') and embed scope in Tasking rollback directives to prevent downstream misinterpretation.

REVISION 2 (Provenance Schema): Document the provenance record structure (source context → target, timestamp, actor, rationale) with examples. Define retention policy (hot audit log duration, cold archive strategy) and indexing strategy (by mission ID, timestamp, parameter type, actor) to ensure audit queries remain performant as logs grow.

REVISION 3 (Phase 1 & Phase 2 Latency): Add performance requirements for context extraction (Phase 1) and validation (Phase 2) latency. If real-time re-baselining is required, specify fast-path derivation for non-critical parameters and caching strategy for rule evaluation. Document acceptable latency bounds and fallback behavior if Phase 2 validation exceeds bounds.

REVISION 4 (Rollback Semantics & Safety): Define explicit rollback scope and interaction with Safety Services. Specify whether rollback triggers SafetyAlert notifications, whether in-flight tasks are aborted or allowed to complete, and how Vehicle and Teleoperation are notified. Add test cases for rollback scenarios (e.g., rollback during active mission, rollback with in-flight tasks, rollback with concurrent SafetyAlert updates).

REVISION 5 (Multi-Instance Consistency): If NGHE Solution is deployed in multiple instances (redundancy, multi-site), specify baseline registry synchronization mechanism (primary-replica, distributed consensus, eventual consistency). Document consistency guarantees and conflict resolution strategy. Add acceptance test for multi-instance baseline consistency.

REVISION 6 (Acceptance Criteria Mapping): Explicitly map each acceptance criterion to NGHE Solution capabilities: (a) 'Documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs' → provenance tracking and audit log queries. (b) 'History of baselines/calibration updates' → versioned baseline registry and immutable audit trail. (c) 'Alignment with worksite safety plan and operational policies' → Phase 2 validation rules and SafetyAlert re-validation. Add test cases for each mapping.

REVISION 7 (Audit Log Querying API): Define a bounded, schema-versioned audit query API (e.g., query by mission ID, timestamp range, parameter type, actor). Document query performance SLAs and caching strategy. Prevent uncontrolled query patterns that could leak implementation details or degrade performance.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass with high confidence (0.92 containment score, HIGH verification feasibility, CLEAR allocation). However, the gate results expose seven critical revisions that must be addressed before decomposition can proceed safely. These revisions are not minor polish—they directly impact safety-critical interface contracts, provenance auditability, rollback semantics, and multi-instance consistency. Given the HIGH safety criticality of this system, decomposing further without resolving these revisions would propagate ambiguity and risk into lower-level sub-FRs. The recommended approach is to ZIGZAG_DOWN by first executing the seven revisions at the current level (FR/DP/IM), then re-validating the gates, and only then deriving next-level sub-FRs with full clarity on interface contracts, latency bounds, and rollback behavior.

revise.why: DP2 + DP4 are logically sound but lack sufficient detail in seven critical areas: (1) Interface contract formalization (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert schema versioning and error handling); (2) Provenance schema documentation with retention and indexing strategy; (3) Phase 1 & Phase 2 latency performance requirements and fast-path caching; (4) Rollback scope semantics and SafetyAlert interaction; (5) Multi-instance baseline registry synchronization mechanism; (6) Explicit mapping of acceptance criteria to NGHE capabilities; (7) Bounded audit query API specification. These details must be embedded in the DP before sub-FRs can be reliably derived, because they define the boundary conditions and interface contracts that sub-level modules will depend on.

assumptions: NGHE Solution is deployed as a single authoritative instance (or multi-instance deployment follows primary-replica or distributed consensus pattern with explicit synchronization guarantees).
Telemetry inbound interface provides worksite mission context in a well-defined, versioned schema.
SafetyAlert bidirectional interface is governed by explicit trigger conditions that prevent re-entrant validation cycles.
Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can interpret and execute Tasking directives with unambiguous rollback scope semantics.
Audit log retention policy and indexing strategy are feasible within operational storage and query performance constraints.
Phase 1 context extraction and Phase 2 validation latency can be bounded to acceptable real-time or near-real-time thresholds.
Rollback directives do not create circular dependencies or safety-critical race conditions with in-flight tasks.
open_risks: CRITICAL: Rollback scope ambiguity (future targets vs. abort in-flight tasks vs. advisory only) could cause Vehicle and Teleoperation to misinterpret directives, leading to inconsistent mission state or safety violations. Mitigation: Embed explicit rollback scope and timestamp in Tasking messages; add test cases for rollback scenarios.

CRITICAL: SafetyAlert bidirectional feedback loop could create re-entrant validation cycles if Safety Services uses NGHE audit alerts to re-trigger constraint updates. Mitigation: Define explicit trigger conditions and prevent re-entrant cycles; add acceptance test for feedback loop containment.

HIGH: Multi-instance NGHE deployments could fragment baseline registry consistency if synchronization mechanism is not explicitly specified. Mitigation: Enforce primary-replica or distributed consensus pattern with documented consistency guarantees.
HIGH: Audit log query patterns could become uncontrolled, leaking implementation details or degrading performance. Mitigation: Provide bounded, schema-versioned audit query API with documented SLAs.

MODERATE: Phase 1 & Phase 2 latency could exceed acceptable bounds during high-frequency re-baselining (e.g., frequent SafetyAlert updates). Mitigation: Specify fast-path derivation for non-critical parameters and caching strategy; add latency performance requirements.

MODERATE: Telemetry and Tasking interface contracts lack explicit schema versioning and error handling, risking downstream misinterpretation of baseline changes. Mitigation: Formalize interface specifications with versioning, message formats, and error handling.

LOW: Provenance tracking retention policy and indexing strategy not yet specified; could impact audit log scalability. Mitigation: Document retention policy (hot/cold archive), indexing strategy (by mission ID, timestamp, parameter type, actor), and query performance SLAs.
evidence_needed: Formalized Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert interface specifications with schema versioning, message formats, error handling, and examples.
Documented provenance record structure with examples, retention policy (hot audit log duration, cold archive strategy), and indexing strategy.
Performance requirements for Phase 1 context extraction and Phase 2 validation latency; fast-path derivation strategy for non-critical parameters; caching strategy for rule evaluation.
Explicit rollback scope semantics (e.g., 'future targets only', 'abort in-flight tasks', 'advisory only') embedded in Tasking rollback directives; test cases for rollback scenarios.

Baseline registry synchronization mechanism specification (primary-replica, distributed consensus, or eventual consistency) with consistency guarantees and conflict resolution strategy; acceptance test for multi-instance consistency.
Explicit mapping of each acceptance criterion to NGHE Solution capabilities with test cases.
Bounded, schema-versioned audit query API specification with query performance SLAs and caching strategy.
Test cases for SafetyAlert feedback loop containment, rollback during active mission, rollback with in-flight tasks, and rollback with concurrent SafetyAlert updates.
